heading: Omitted
label: 2821 to 2826 Omitted
notes: CodificationSections 2821 to 2826 expired, not later than the thirtieth day after , pursuant to .June 30, 1975section 2823 of this titleSection 2821, , , , declared that it was the purpose of this chapter to establish a commission to make studies and recommendations directed at providing a more effective system for formulation and implementation of foreign policy.Pub. L. 92–352, title VI, § 60186 Stat. 497July 13, 1972Section 2822, , , , established the commission, known as the Commission on the Organization of the Government for the Conduct of Foreign Policy.Pub. L. 92–352, title VI, § 60286 Stat. 497July 13, 1972Section 2823, , , ; , , , related to duties of the commission, required a comprehensive report be submitted to the President and to Congress not later than , and provided that the commission cease to exist on the thirtieth day after the report was filed.Pub. L. 92–352, title VI, § 60386 Stat. 497Pub. L. 93–126, § 487 Stat. 452July 13, 1972Oct. 18, 1973June 30, 1975Section 2824, , , , related to powers of the commission.Pub. L. 92–352, title VI, § 60486 Stat. 498July 13, 1972Section 2825, , , , related to appointment and compensation of personnel and the services of experts and consultants.Pub. L. 92–352, title VI, § 60586 Stat. 498July 13, 1972Section 2826, , , , authorized sums as necessary to carry out the provisions of this chapter.Pub. L. 92–352, title VI, § 60686 Stat. 499July 13, 1972
